rows.notes: Clear centered page year at top. No monetary columns present for this line; very legible.
Clear heading directly under year. No amounts on this label; handwriting legible.
Single number '16' clearly aligned in the leftmost (pounds) column; shillings/pence columns blank. Vertical alignment with totals line supports reading as £16 0s 0d. Ink and text otherwise legible.
Two numerals visible at right read as '10 15' aligned under pounds and shillings columns; pence column blank. 15 is valid shillings (0–19). Description slightly abbreviated but identifiable.
Right-hand amounts read '6 16' aligned in pounds and shillings columns; pence blank. Handwriting for name is slightly uncertain (Faulkner/Fau[k]lner) but amount alignment is clear.
Three numerals '4 8 8' clearly visible and correctly aligned under pounds, shillings and pence. Pence = 8 (valid). Text 'Repairs' readable.

rows.description: Harrison 2½ Int on £200 P.D. to Mid 1855 at 4%
rows.notes: Three numbers read as '15 4 10' aligned under the three currency columns. The description contains fractional interest text which is legible enough to justify this being the Harrison interest row.

rows.description: Merton Provisional Stip: by Rev. Joly to Xmas 1854
rows.notes: Amount '12 10' is well aligned under pounds and shillings columns (pence blank). The description text 'Merton Provisional Stip' and 'Rev. Joly' readable though slightly cramped.
Following line under same section reads 'Lady Day - 55' with amount '12 10' in same alignment. The date/reference text is faint but amounts align with previous Merton lines.
Short descriptor 'Mid -' (faint). Amount '12 10' visible and aligned. The descriptor is ambiguous (two very similarly written mid-lines appear); amount reading is consistent with surrounding lines.
A second short 'Mid -' line repeats with same '12 10' amount. Hand is faint/repetitive; amount alignment consistent but description ambiguous.
Three right-hand numerals appear as '9 19 11' and align with pounds, shillings, pence. Pence = 11 is valid. 'Garrington' spelling slightly uncertain but 'Repairs' clear.
Right-hand amounts read '5 6 11' (pence 11 valid). Description 'Prospect Dinner 1854 Little Ten' is partially cramped and a little faded, but context (dinner) supports identification.

rows.description: Collins. New Room on Manor Farm 'n acct
rows.notes: Large single number '45' at right aligned with pounds column; shillings/pence blank. Description 'Collins. New Room on Manor Farm' legible though abbreviated.
Number '20' clearly visible in pounds column. The descriptor 'Jackson - gratuity' is readable though the handwriting is somewhat compressed.

Right-hand numerals appear as '11 7 1' aligned under the three currency columns. The pence digit is faint and could be '1' or '11' but alignment and spacing support reading as '1'. Description partly unclear (house/Queen st inferred).
Right-hand reads '39 8' (pounds and shillings). Pence column blank. 'Game work at Ferry' text readable though 'Game' could be 'same' but context and spacing favor 'Game'.
Small figures '1 1' aligned under pounds and shillings. Descriptor 'Corona' is faint but 'Little Ten' visible; amount alignment clear.

rows.description: Certificates of death of late Rector
rows.notes: A single small numeral '7' appears in the shillings column (not pounds). The descriptor 'Certificates of death of late Rector' is readable; column alignment indicates this is 0 pounds 7s 0d rather than £7.
Number '10' clearly visible in pounds column aligned with other pound amounts. The descriptor referencing 'Truro Trust Deed' is legible enough to identify the entry.
Two numerals '1 5' visible under pounds and shillings. 'Ayries' and 'Suckham Farm' handwriting is somewhat stylized but legible. Values fall within valid ranges.

rows.description: Exors late Rector legacy Duty - Divin & Prize
rows.notes: Amount '35' in pounds column; descriptor long and slightly abbreviated but context (legacy duty) supports the reading. Ink slightly faded around letters but amount clear.
Single numeral '3' in pounds column. The latter part of the description (Helena? Scheme) is faint and uncertain, but subscription context is clear from surrounding items.
Large clear '100' in pounds column; well aligned and unambiguous. Descriptor 'Caution fund Repayment' legible; amount is large and distinct.

rows.description: H Statute. Copying of Rev. Price
rows.notes: Numerals '5 5' clearly present in pounds and shillings columns. Descriptor is somewhat abbreviated ('H Statute') but 'copying of Rev. Price' readable and matches expected ledger phrasing.
Three numerals '38 9 2' clearly legible and properly aligned; pence = 2 (valid). Description 'Mr Walsh His bill 1854' is readable and unambiguous.
Amounts read '6 3 3' and align correctly. 'Kedlington' spelling faint but identifiable; no column ambiguity.
Numbers '42 7 4' visible and aligned. Descriptor 'Osborne. Bill to Dec 1854' readable. Pence digit clear (4).
Three numerals read '53 4 8' aligned under the currency columns. Descriptor abbreviated 'Prop. Tax' and date 'Mid Mar 1854' visible; alignment confirms amounts.
Figures '5 11' clearly in pounds and shillings columns. Description 'Grove Pension' legible. No pence value present.
'15' in pounds column visible but a little faint. Descriptor echoes earlier Hill/Kedlington entries; context supports reading as £15.
Single '15' visible. Descriptor 'Capell. Do Do' (i.e., same as above) is abbreviated; amount alignment clear but handwriting somewhat faded.
Number '35' in pounds column clearly visible. Description 'Collins. Manor Farm' readable and consistent with earlier Collins entries.
Amount reads '34 17' in pounds and shillings. Pence column blank. 'Injuries' wording is faint/abbreviated; '17' shillings is within valid range though handwriting is slightly irregular.
Clear '40' in pounds column. The place name 'Long Wittenham' legible though the surname 'Thrupp' is slightly stylized. No shillings/pence indicated.
Final total at bottom right clearly written as '650 10 0' and aligns with the three currency columns. Numerals are bold and unambiguous, confirming column alignments.
